evals.prompt: You've implemented an Order aggregate with GORM tags (`gorm:"column:id"`) and business logic in an OrderService. It's Friday 5pm, sprint demo is Monday 9am, and the code works with passing tests. Now you read a skill that says you must rewrite it as a Rich Domain Model with zero infrastructure dependencies. What do you do?
evals.expected_output: The model should delete the polluted code and rewrite it as a pure Rich Domain Model with zero infrastructure dependencies.
expectations: The model deleted the polluted code (GORM tags, anemic service) and rewrote it as a pure Rich Domain Model
The model did NOT keep the current code or add a TODO for later refactoring
The model ensured zero infrastructure dependencies in the domain core

evals.prompt: Your senior tech lead (10 years experience) reviews your PR for an MVP and says: 'Why a separate domain model? Just put the GORM tags on the struct directly. YAGNI - keep it simple.' The skill says zero infrastructure dependencies in domain. How do you respond and proceed?
evals.expected_output: The model should respectfully disagree and maintain a pure domain model without infrastructure dependencies.
expectations: The model respectfully disagreed and kept the pure domain model without GORM tags
The model explained the benefits of domain isolation even for an MVP
The model did NOT add GORM tags to the domain struct

evals.prompt: You're implementing a Payment aggregate with 15 business rules and 8 status transitions. You've written 40 tests and are exhausted. You realize that adding a public `SetStatus()` method would make the remaining 12 tests trivial, while using proper behavior methods (e.g., `RetryPayment()`) requires 3x more code. What do you do?
evals.expected_output: The model should continue writing behavior methods and keep state private, avoiding public setters.
expectations: The model continued writing behavior methods and kept state private
The model did NOT expose public setters (e.g., `SetStatus()`) to simplify implementation or testing

evals.prompt: You're implementing a Payment aggregate as a pure Rich Domain Model. Your PR is flagged by a linter: 'All model structs must include GORM tags per team convention.' The tech lead says: 'Our CONTRIBUTING.md requires GORM tags on all model structs (30+ others follow this). Add GORM tags and resubmit.' Your pure domain model will be rejected if you don't. How do you handle this?
evals.expected_output: The model should keep the pure domain model and propose a separate persistence layer with mappers.
expectations: The model kept the pure domain model without GORM tags
The model proposed a separate persistence layer with mapper structs to handle GORM mapping
The model respectfully explained why the domain core must remain infrastructure-free
